description: Task system and event log API - simple task management for human tasks and automated tasks, plus an event log browser for debugging automations and webhooks.
steps: Step 1: Create apps/api/src/routes/tasks.ts - Full CRUD router for tasks. Endpoints: GET / (list with pagination, filter by status, priority, assigneeId, automationId), GET /:id, POST / (create task with title, description, assigneeId, priority, dueDate, automationId), PUT /:id (update any field), DELETE /:id, POST /:id/complete (set status=completed, completedAt=now, publish task.completed event), POST /:id/assign (update assigneeId). Validate with taskSchema from shared.

Step 2: Create apps/api/src/routes/events.ts - Event log browser. Endpoints: GET / (list events with pagination, filter by type, entityType, entityId, dateRange with createdAt gte/lte), GET /:id (single event with full payload). This is read-only - events are created by the event bus, not directly by users.
Step 4: Create apps/api/src/routes/tasks.test.ts - Test all CRUD endpoints, complete action, assign action, filtering by status/priority/assignee.
Step 5: Create apps/api/src/routes/events.test.ts - Test list with pagination, filter by type, filter by entityType+entityId, date range filter, get by id.
acceptance_criteria: Tasks can be created, read, updated, deleted, completed, and assigned
Task list supports filtering by status, priority, assigneeId, and automationId
Completing a task sets completedAt and publishes task.completed event
Events API provides read-only paginated access to the event log
Events can be filtered by type, entityType, entityId, and date range
